§6Zirconium & Tantalum§f

§aRare earth metals essential for fuel rod casings and high-end capacitors!§f

§6Zirconium§f (atomic number 40):
• Rare earth metal with high corrosion resistance, high melting point, and overall strength
• Production: centrifuge/acidize rare earth ore for splinters; cyclotron (strontium + lithium); depth ore (must be destroyed by explosion to collect)
§bUses§f:
  • RBMK Fuel Rod§b casing§f — skin of every RBMK fuel rod
  • Zirconium Fast Breeders (ZFBs)§f — binders for bismuth/Pu/ZR breeder rods
  • PWR Fuel Rods§f and §bNeutron Reflectors§f
  • ZIRNOX Fuel Rods§f
  • Catalytic Filters§f — for carbon monoxide gas masks
  • Zirconium Pants§f

§6Tantalum (Tantalium)§f (atomic number 73):
• Very rare natural metal, production is a long and complex multi-step process
§bProduction Chain§f:
  1. Find §bColtan Ore§f deposit (singular large deposit within 1,000 blocks; use "coltass" to locate)
  2. Mine with fortune enchantment; shred for Crushed Coltan
  3. Chemical Plant: 2 Crushed Coltan + Coal Powder + 250 mB H₂O₂ + 500 mB Liquid H₂ → Purified Tantalite + Niobium byproduct
  4. Chemical Plant: Purified Tantalite + Fluorite + 1,000 mB Natural Gas + 500 mB Liquid O₂ → Pandemonium(III)tantalite Solution
  5. Chemical Plant: 1,000 mB solution + 500 mB H₂O₂ → Tantalum Polycrystal
  6. Shred polycrystal → powder → smelt → ingot
• Also from: PUREX reprocessing (schrabidium Watz pellets) and SILEX (Sa-326 long-lived waste)
§bUses§f:
  • Tantalum Capacitors§f — crucial for bomb control units, quantum computers, and 528 Mode capacitor boards
  • Advanced Control Units§f

§eTip§f: Zirconium is §eeverywhere§f in reactor fuel rods. Tantalum is a §eone-time investment§f — set up the full coltan processing chain and stockpile enough for capacitors. You don't need continuous production once your capacitor needs are met!
